name: design-md
description: Analyze Stitch projects and synthesize a semantic design system into DESIGN.md files
capabilities: Extracts color palettes from screen assets and assigns semantic names
Translates CSS/Tailwind values into descriptive design language
Documents typography hierarchy with natural language descriptions
Generates DESIGN.md files following semantic design system format
Maps component patterns to design tokens with functional descriptions
author: google-labs-code
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly flagged hex color codes as weak cryptographic algorithms, markdown backticks as shell execution, and Stitch MCP tool names as C2 keywords. This is documentation-only content that generates design system files using authorized API calls.
valueStatement: Design systems are often trapped in technical CSS values that are hard to translate into prompts. This skill analyzes visual assets and translates them into natural language design documentation that ensures consistent UI generation.
limitations: Requires access to Stitch MCP Server for project and screen data
Cannot create new design systems from scratch without existing reference screens
Output quality depends on completeness of source screen metadata

useCases.targetUser: Designers working with Stitch to build UI components
useCases.title: Create consistent design language for a new Stitch project
useCases.description: Generate a comprehensive DESIGN.md that serves as the source of truth for prompting Stitch to create new screens that match your existing design language.

useCases.targetUser: Design system engineers and frontend developers
useCases.title: Document existing design patterns for team collaboration
useCases.description: Analyze completed screens and produce human-readable design documentation that helps team members understand and extend the design system.

useCases.title: Create reference documentation for AI-assisted design iteration
useCases.description: Build a semantic design guide that enables precise prompts for incremental improvements to existing screens while maintaining design consistency.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Design Documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the [Project Name] project and generate a DESIGN.md file documenting the design system from the Home screen.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick start for documenting a project's visual design

promptTemplates.title: Specific Screen Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Retrieve the [Screen Name] screen from the [Project Name] project. Analyze the visual design and create a comprehensive DESIGN.md that covers color palette, typography, component styles, and layout principles.
promptTemplates.scenario: Documenting a specific screen's design language

promptTemplates.title: Design Token Extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract all design tokens from the [Project Name] project including colors, spacing values, border-radius patterns, and shadow styles. Generate semantic descriptions for each token with functional roles.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a complete token inventory for a design system

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Screen Design Synthesis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze multiple screens from the [Project Name] project to identify consistent design patterns. Synthesize a unified DESIGN.md that captures the core design language while documenting component-specific variations.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building comprehensive design documentation from multiple screens

bestPractices: Use semantic color names that convey character and purpose (e.g., 'Deep Muted Teal-Navy' rather than just 'Dark Blue')
Always include both descriptive names and hex codes for colors to enable precise AI prompting
Document functional roles for each design element to guide consistent usage across screens
antiPatterns: Using technical CSS class names without translation (e.g., 'rounded-xl' instead of 'generously rounded corners')
Omitting color codes and relying only on descriptive names
Failing to explain the functional purpose of design elements

faq.question: What is DESIGN.md and why do I need it?
faq.answer: DESIGN.md serves as the source of truth for prompting Stitch to generate new screens. It translates technical design values into natural language that Stitch interprets for consistent UI creation.

faq.question: What access do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need access to the Stitch MCP Server and a Stitch project with at least one designed screen to analyze.

faq.question: What formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill works with HTML, CSS, and Tailwind-based designs. It extracts Tailwind classes and custom CSS to create design tokens.

faq.question: How long does documentation take?
faq.answer: The skill typically completes analysis and documentation in under 2 minutes depending on screen complexity.

faq.question: Can I update DESIGN.md as my project evolves?
faq.answer: Yes. Re-run the skill on updated screens to generate revised documentation that reflects design changes.

faq.question: What makes good semantic design documentation?
faq.answer: Good documentation uses evocative, human-readable descriptions for all design elements and always includes functional context for each component.
